title: HIV Viral Load: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
t your immune system and help prevent you from getting AIDS. Medicines that treat HIV are called antiretroviral therapy (ART). ART can't get rid of HIV completely, so you'll need to take medicines for the rest of your life. But ART can control HIV and help you live a longer, healthier life.
Keeping your HIV viral load low is important for lowering your risk of complications with this type of infection. Having regular HIV viral load tests is an important part of making sure your HIV medicines are keeping your viral load low, so you stay healthy.
Other names: nucleic acid testing, NAT, nucleic acid amplification test, NAAT, HIV PCR, RNA Test, HIV quantification
What is it used for? 
An HIV viral load test is mainly used after you're diagnosed with HIV to:
Guide decisions about your treatment 
Check how well your HIV medicines are working 
Watch for any changes in your HIV infection, such as to see if the infection is getting worse, is stable, or getting better 
HIV viral load testing is also used to test newborn babies when they are born to a person who has HIV. That's because HIV can be passed to a fetus during pregnancy , a baby during childbirth, and through breast milk.
In certain cases, an HIV viral load test may be used to diagnose HIV. Usually, HIV screening tests Â are used first. That's because HIV viral load tests are expensive. But viral load tests can find HIV sooner
